description:
GAP that LANDED: bl-f813 (CLOSED, delivered 9e21a82) shipped the bl-delivery sibling plugin WITHOUT its §16 doctor hook. Its self-description is PROTOCOL_JSON = {"protocol":[1],"ops":["claim","unclaim","drop","close"]} (no 'doctor'), and delivery::dispatch has no doctor arm (falls to _ => Ok(())). So the §16 CODE-worktree drift class is implemented NOWHERE: base doctor (bl-d024, delivered) deliberately cannot see it (base reads only clones/<enc>/changes/, the §8 ephemeral op scratch; the code worktree lives in the delivery plugin's own XDG territory at a derived path = f(binding,id) that base never computes nor opens), and the plugin that owns that territory didn't wire the check. bl-f813's scope line enumerated only claim.post/close.pre/close.post/unclaim+drop.post — the doctor hook was omitted, and the impl followed the scope.

Spec bl-2e26 §16, verbatim: 'the delivery plugin audits CODE worktrees — a claimed ball whose worktree_path(binding, id) (§11) is missing (failed claim.post or a hand-deletion), or a closed/dropped ball whose worktree still exists (failed close.post/drop.post teardown). Base balls never derives that path nor opens the project repo.' The design ball bl-2a77 also flagged this: 'the plugin-owned worktree is a second drift class, likely a plugin-contributed doctor check.'

SCOPE:
1. Add 'doctor' to bl-delivery's PROTOCOL_JSON ops list so 'bl install' will wire it into the doctor hook AND validate it (install checks Protocol::handles(op)).
2. Add a doctor/<phase> arm to delivery::dispatch performing the audit: for the doctor read op, cwd = operating/ (diffless), so the hook enumerates tasks/ and partitions by claimant (the §3 occupancy field). For each CLAIMED id, derive worktree_path(binding,id) and flag MISSING as drift (fix: 'bl prime' — idempotent re-materialize). For each leftover worktree on the project repo with NO live claim, flag as orphan (fix: the named 'git worktree remove <path>' — human runs it, may hold uncommitted work). binding (incl. invocation_path → project repo) is on the §7 wire.
3. Reads have no return channel (§7): the hook prints findings to stdout, nothing parsed back — same contract base doctor::audit uses. 100% coverage + <300 lines/file.
title: Delivery plugin: §16 doctor hook (code-worktree drift audit)
